---
title: "OpenGWASDB vs besdq — Performance Comparison"
subtitle: "chr1 × 100 UKB analyses · dense observed-only store"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    theme: cosmo
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

HERE = Path(".")

def load_json(path):
    p = Path(path)
    return json.loads(p.read_text()) if p.exists() else None

bench    = load_json(HERE / "opengwasdb_vcf_ukb_chr1_benchmark.json")
baseline = load_json(HERE / "opengwasdb_vcf_ukb_chr1_array_benchmark.json")
besdq    = load_json(HERE / "besdq_ukb_chr1_benchmark.json")

PATTERNS = ["regional", "phewas", "tophits", "bulk", "random_lookup"]

def timing(data, pattern):
    """Extract median_ms from opengwasdb benchmark JSON for a pattern."""
    if data is None:
        return None
    for t in data.get("timings", []):
        if t["query"] == pattern:
            return t["median_ms"]
    return None

def besdq_timing(variant="zstd_bitshuffle", pattern=None):
    if besdq is None or variant not in besdq:
        return None
    return besdq[variant].get(pattern, {}).get("median_ms")

def ratio(a, b):
    if a is None or b is None or b == 0:
        return None
    return a / b

def fmt_ms(v, decimals=2):
    if v is None:
        return "n/a"
    return f"{v:.{decimals}f} ms"

def fmt_ratio(r, threshold=2.0):
    if r is None:
        return "n/a"
    warn = r > threshold
    s = f"{r:.2f}×"
    if warn:
        return f'<span style="color:#c0392b;font-weight:bold">{s}</span>'
    return f'<span style="color:#27ae60">{s}</span>'

def fmt_speedup(before, after):
    if before is None or after is None or after == 0:
        return "n/a"
    s = before / after
    return f'<span style="color:#2980b9;font-weight:bold">{s:.1f}×</span>'

def md_table(headers, rows):
    sep = "|" + "|".join(["---"] * len(headers)) + "|"
    head = "| " + " | ".join(headers) + " |"
    body = "\n".join("| " + " | ".join(str(c) for c in row) + " |" for row in rows)
    return f"{head}\n{sep}\n{body}"
```

## Overview

This document compares query and storage performance between **OpenGWASDB** (this project)
and **besdq** (the prototype it is intended to replace) on the same source dataset:
100 UKB GWAS-VCF analyses covering chromosome 1 (~762 K variants, hg38).

Both systems store float16 z-scores and betas in zarr arrays with zstd+bitshuffle
compression. Key architectural differences are noted where they affect comparability.

---

## Dataset

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
if bench:
    d = bench["dataset"]
    rows = [
        ("Source", "100 × UKB GWAS-VCF (hg19)", "same VCFs"),
        ("Reference assembly", "GRCh38 (hg38)", "GRCh38 (hg38)"),
        ("Variants retained", f"{d['n_variants']:,}", "762,488"),
        ("Variant difference", "−408 (liftover failures, 0.05%)", "—"),
        ("Analyses", str(d["n_analyses"]), "100"),
    ]
    print(md_table(["Property", "OpenGWASDB", "besdq"], rows))
else:
    print("_Benchmark JSON not found._")
```

---

## Build

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
if bench:
    b = bench["build"]
    bs = b.get("build_seconds")
    build_str = f"{bs:.0f} s" if bs else "not measured"
    rows = [
        ("Build time", build_str, "not measured"),
        ("VCF reader", "bcftools (subprocess)", "cyvcf2"),
        ("Liftover", "hg19→hg38 inline (pyliftover)", "pre-lifted source"),
        ("Build strategy", "two-pass (variant union then fill)", "one-pass"),
    ]
    print(md_table(["Property", "OpenGWASDB", "besdq"], rows))
```

OpenGWASDB's build time includes the hg19→hg38 liftover pass on all 100 VCFs and writes
a tabix-indexed variant axis alongside the zarr arrays. besdq assumes pre-lifted input and
does not write a tabix index.

---

## Storage

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
if bench:
    mb = bench["storage"]["store_mb"]
    rows = [
        ("OpenGWASDB", f"**{mb:.0f} MB**", "`z`, `se`, top-hit index, ALID index", "(n_analyses, 1000)", "float16"),
        ("besdq zstd+bitshuffle", "233 MB", "`beta`, `zscore`, sig indices", "(1000, n_analyses)", "float16"),
        ("besdq raw float16", "310 MB", "same", "(1000, n_analyses)", "float16"),
    ]
    print(md_table(["Store", "Size", "Arrays stored", "Chunk shape", "dtype"], rows))
```

OpenGWASDB stores two effect arrays (`z` and `se`) plus a pre-computed top-hit sparse
index and an mmap'd ALID search index. besdq stores `beta` and `zscore` plus pre-computed
significance offset arrays. Chunk shape is clipped to actual array dimensions at build
time (ADR-0021), so both stores have effective chunks of (1000, 100) for this dataset.

---

## Query timings

All OpenGWASDB timings are median over 10 repetitions after one warm-up call.
besdq timings are from `ukb-chr1_zarr_benchmark.json` (same methodology).
Times in milliseconds.

### Current (post-optimisation)

Fixes applied: zarr chunk shape clipping (ADR-0021), `range_indices()` (issue 028),
mmap'd ALID sidecar (issues 029–030).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
if bench and besdq:
    rows = []
    for p in PATTERNS:
        ours = timing(bench, p)
        bzstd = besdq_timing("zstd_bitshuffle", p)
        braw  = besdq_timing("raw_float16", p)
        r = ratio(ours, bzstd)
        rows.append((p, fmt_ms(ours), fmt_ms(bzstd), fmt_ms(braw), fmt_ratio(r)))
    print(md_table(
        ["Pattern", "OpenGWASDB", "besdq zstd", "besdq raw", "ODB / besdq"],
        rows,
    ))
elif bench is None:
    print("_Post-fix benchmark JSON not found — re-run benchmark after implementing issues 028–030._")
```

### Before optimisations (baseline)

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
if baseline and besdq:
    rows = []
    for p in PATTERNS:
        ours = timing(baseline, p)
        bzstd = besdq_timing("zstd_bitshuffle", p)
        r = ratio(ours, bzstd)
        rows.append((p, fmt_ms(ours), fmt_ms(bzstd), fmt_ratio(r)))
    print(md_table(
        ["Pattern", "OpenGWASDB (pre-fix)", "besdq zstd", "ODB / besdq"],
        rows,
    ))
```

### Improvement from optimisations

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
if bench and baseline:
    rows = []
    for p in PATTERNS:
        before = timing(baseline, p)
        after  = timing(bench, p)
        rows.append((p, fmt_ms(before), fmt_ms(after), fmt_speedup(before, after)))
    print(md_table(["Pattern", "Before", "After", "Speedup"], rows))
```

---

## Query pattern definitions

| Pattern | What it reads | Result contract |
|---|---|---|
| **regional** | All finite associations in a 1 Mb genomic window, all analyses | sparse dict |
| **phewas** | One variant × all 100 analyses | sparse dict |
| **tophits** | All GW-significant (p < 5×10⁻⁸) associations for one analysis | sparse dict |
| **bulk** | All finite associations for one analysis (~762 K variants) | sparse dict |
| **random_lookup** | 100 random variants × 10 random analyses | sparse dict |

### Comparability notes

**(a) Regional — different benchmark windows.**
OpenGWASDB's benchmark selects chr1:4–5 Mb; besdq's benchmark uses chr1:100–101 Mb.
```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
if bench:
    s = bench.get("selection", {})
    rc = bench["timings"][0].get("result_count", "?")
    n_vars = rc // bench["dataset"]["n_analyses"] if rc else "?"
    print(f"Our window has ~{n_vars:,} variants; besdq's has ~2 751. "
          f"The `range_indices()` fix eliminates per-variant Python object allocation "
          f"(previously 51.6 ms for 5 463 variants); the remaining gap tracks the "
          f"variant count difference.")
```

**(b) Bulk — both systems read two arrays.**
besdq's `query_bulk` reads `beta` + `zscore` (2 zarr columns); OpenGWASDB reads `z` + `se`
(2 zarr columns) and applies an `np.isfinite` mask. The gap is not explained by
array count; it likely reflects I/O timing variance between benchmark runs.

**(c) Random lookup — inherent two-array cost.**
besdq's `query_random_lookup` reads one array (`beta`); OpenGWASDB reads two (`z` and
`se`) per the sparse array contract (ADR-0020). A ~2× gap is therefore expected.
The mmap'd ALID index eliminates sequential tabix fetch overhead (~15 ms previously).

---

## Root-cause summary

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
if bench and baseline:
    data = []
    for p in PATTERNS:
        before = timing(baseline, p)
        after  = timing(bench, p)
        bzstd  = besdq_timing("zstd_bitshuffle", p)
        r_before = ratio(before, bzstd)
        r_after  = ratio(after, bzstd)
        data.append((p, before, after, bzstd, r_before, r_after))

    rows = []
    for p, before, after, bzstd, rb, ra in data:
        if before is None or after is None:
            continue
        speedup = before / after if after else None
        change = "—"
        if speedup and speedup > 1.1:
            change = f"**{speedup:.1f}× faster**"
        rows.append((
            p,
            fmt_ms(before),
            fmt_ratio(rb) if rb else "n/a",
            fmt_ms(after),
            fmt_ratio(ra) if ra else "n/a",
            change,
        ))
    print(md_table(
        ["Pattern", "Before", "vs besdq (before)", "After", "vs besdq (after)", "Change"],
        rows,
    ))
```

---

## What OpenGWASDB adds beyond besdq

| Feature | OpenGWASDB | besdq |
|---|---|---|
| Reference assembly liftover | hg19→hg38 inline at build time | requires pre-lifted input |
| Variant axis | tabix-indexed TSV (range queries, rsid aliases) | numpy key array (position lookup only) |
| ALID index | mmap'd sorted binary search, O(log n), ~0 startup | in-memory dict, O(1), ~1.5 GB at 12 M variants |
| Effect statistics | z + se (both stored and returned) | beta + zscore (both stored) |
| Query result contract | sparse `dict[str, np.ndarray]` (finite cells only) | dense numpy arrays (NaN for absent) |
| Top-hit index | pre-computed, threshold-parameterised, includes se | pre-computed z/beta, no se |
| Store validation | full format check at open time | none |
| CLI | yes | no |

---

## Provenance

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
lines = []
if bench:
    lines.append(f"- OpenGWASDB post-fix timings: `opengwasdb_vcf_ukb_chr1_benchmark.json` "
                 f"(built {bench['build'].get('build_seconds', '?'):.0f}s, "
                 f"{bench['dataset']['n_variants']:,} variants × {bench['dataset']['n_analyses']} analyses)")
if baseline:
    lines.append(f"- OpenGWASDB pre-fix timings: `opengwasdb_vcf_ukb_chr1_array_benchmark.json`")
lines.append("- besdq timings: `besdq_ukb_chr1_benchmark.json` (copied from besdq repo by `benchmarks/README.md` step)")
lines.append("- besdq store: `ukb-chr1-zstd.besdz` (zstd+bitshuffle), `ukb-chr1.besdz` (raw float16)")
print("\n".join(lines))
```
